response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine developer-tooling credentials — he built C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI (an actual IDE), authored test runners and assertion libraries, and currently works on MCP code generators. However, the role's core required stack is essentially absent: there is no substantive Java or Kotlin experience beyond incidental use, no Spring or enterprise-framework work, and no demonstrated JVM depth. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, making him a stack mismatch for a JVM-tooling IDE team despite a clear affinity for the problem space.
strengths: Direct IDE-internals experience building components for NI LabWindows/CVI
Strong developer-tooling track record (test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators)
Proven low-latency, large-scale systems work handling millions of requests per second
concerns: No meaningful Java/Kotlin or JVM-depth experience — the role's central requirement
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise-framework background
Frequent stack-hopping (TypeScript, DLang, PHP, Swift) with no sustained JVM ecosystem focus
